잠언 13:1.content: A wise son listens to his father’s instruction, but a scoffer doesn’t listen to rebuke.

잠언 13:2.content: By the fruit of his lips, a man enjoys good things, but the unfaithful crave violence.

잠언 13:3.content: He who guards his mouth guards his soul. One who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.

잠언 13:4.content: The soul of the sluggard desires, and has nothing, but the desire of the diligent shall be fully satisfied.

잠언 13:5.content: A righteous man hates lies, but a wicked man brings shame and disgrace.

잠언 13:6.content: Righteousness guards the way of integrity, but wickedness overthrows the sinner.

잠언 13:7.content: There are some who pretend to be rich, yet have nothing. There are some who pretend to be poor, yet have great wealth.

잠언 13:8.content: The ransom of a man’s life is his riches, but the poor hear no threats.

잠언 13:9.content: The light of the righteous shines brightly, but the lamp of the wicked is snuffed out.

잠언 13:10.content: Pride only breeds quarrels, but wisdom is with people who take advice.

잠언 13:11.content: Wealth gained dishonestly dwindles away, but he who gathers by hand makes it grow.

잠언 13:12.content: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but when longing is fulfilled, it is a tree of life.

잠언 13:13.content: Whoever despises instruction will pay for it, but he who respects a command will be rewarded.

잠언 13:14.content: The teaching of the wise is a spring of life, to turn from the snares of death.

잠언 13:15.content: Good understanding wins favor, but the way of the unfaithful is hard.

잠언 13:16.content: Every prudent man acts from knowledge, but a fool exposes folly.

잠언 13:17.content: A wicked messenger falls into trouble, but a trustworthy envoy gains healing.

잠언 13:18.content: Poverty and shame come to him who refuses discipline, but he who heeds correction shall be honored.

잠언 13:19.content: Longing fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but fools detest turning from evil.

잠언 13:20.content: One who walks with wise men grows wise, but a companion of fools suffers harm.

잠언 13:21.content: Misfortune pursues sinners, but prosperity rewards the righteous.

잠언 13:22.content: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children’s children, but the wealth of the sinner is stored for the righteous.

잠언 13:23.content: An abundance of food is in poor people’s fields, but injustice sweeps it away.

잠언 13:24.content: One who spares the rod hates his son, but one who loves him is careful to discipline him.

잠언 13:25.content: The righteous one eats to the satisfying of his soul, but the belly of the wicked goes hungry.
